overall.description: Third-person gameplay footage from GTA V showing a character walking along a commercial city street, crossing at a marked intersection, and continuing past the Sunset Plaza building. The scene features clear sunny weather, urban architecture with modern skyscrapers in the distance, and light pedestrian and vehicle traffic.

segments.scene_description: The character walks along an urban sidewalk bordered by a stone wall on the left and trees on the right. Two pedestrians walk ahead in the same direction as the camera approaches a marked crosswalk intersection.

scene_state.functional_zone: marked pedestrian crosswalk at urban intersection

segments.scene_description: The character crosses the street at a wide intersection with clear visibility. The Sunset Plaza building becomes prominent on the right as a black SUV passes by. The crosswalk features distinctive yellow and red textured curb ramps.

scene_state.functional_zone: commercial sidewalk with Sunset Plaza storefront and tree canopy

segments.scene_description: Having crossed the street, the character walks along the sidewalk past the Sunset Plaza commercial building with its prominent signage and red awnings. The scene features a hedge row on the left, parked cars on the right, and scattered pedestrians walking in the opposite direction.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous camera movement along a sunny city sidewalk that transitions from a tree-lined path to a wide commercial intersection and finally past a prominent plaza. It begins by following a character walking beside a stone wall and urban greenery, moving steadily toward a marked crosswalk. As the character enters the wide intersection, the perspective opens up to reveal the Sunset Plaza building on the right while a black SUV passes through the frame. The scene then shifts as the character completes the crossing, moving past the red awnings and signage of the plaza. Finally, the camera continues along the sidewalk, flanked by a hedge row and parked cars, as pedestrians pass by in the opposite direction.
